scope_note: Curated vendor list for FedRAMP 20x / KSI due diligence in the cmmc-advisor corpus. KSI catalog fields refresh from the public FedRAMP Consolidated Rules JSON; Marketplace authorization fields refresh from the official export; public_trust_center_url and ksi_due_diligence are practitioner checklist items for publicly verifiable checks only.
verification_guidance: KSI indicators and FRR excerpts come from the public FedRAMP Consolidated Rules JSON. Marketplace fields are live at generation time. public_trust_center_url and ksi_due_diligence are corpus-curated checklist items for publicly verifiable due diligence only; they do not prove private KSI validation outcomes. Re-run before vendor SSP citations.

indicators.name: Reviewing All Training
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of relevant cybersecurity education and training is persistently reviewed, including at least general training for all employees, role-specific training for employees in high risk roles, training for development and engineering staff on secure software delivery, and training for staff involved with incident response or disaster recovery.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Logging Changes
indicators.statement: Modifications to the cloud service offering are logged and monitored.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Redeploying vs Modifying
indicators.statement: Changes to machine-based information resources are executed through the redeployment of version controlled resources rather than direct modification wherever reasonable.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Change Procedures
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of documented change management procedures is persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Validating Throughout Deployment
indicators.statement: Persistent testing and validation of changes throughout deployment is automated.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Defining Functionality and Privileges
indicators.statement: The functionality and privileges for infrastructure and services are strictly defined.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.
Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Implementing Best Practices
indicators.statement: The use and configuration of third-party machine-based information resources is persistently compared against the original provider's best practices and guidance.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Minimizing Attack Surface
indicators.statement: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they have a minimal attack surface and that lateral movement is minimized if compromised.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Optimizing for Availability
indicators.statement: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they are appropriately optimized for high availability and rapid recovery.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Restricting Network Traffic
indicators.statement: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they are appropriately configured to limit inbound and outbound network traffic.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Protections
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of protection against denial of service attacks and other unwanted activity for machine-based information resources is persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Using Logical Networking
indicators.statement: Logical networking and related capabilities are used and persistently reviewed to enforce traffic flow controls.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Automating Account Management
indicators.statement: The lifecycle and privileges of all accounts, roles, and groups are securely managed using automation.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Adopting Passwordless Methods
indicators.statement: Secure passwordless methods are used for user authentication and authorization when feasible, otherwise strong passwords with phishing-resistant MFA is used.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Ensuring Least Privilege
indicators.statement: Identity and access management measures are used and persistently reviewed to ensure each user or device can only access the resources they need.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Authorizing Just-in-Time
indicators.statement: A least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time security authorization model is used and persistently reviewed for all user and non-user accounts and services.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Securing Non-User Authentication
indicators.statement: Appropriately secure authentication methods are used and persistently reviewed for non-user accounts and services.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Responding to Suspicious Activity
indicators.statement: Accounts with privileged access are disabled or otherwise secured in response to suspicious activity.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Generating After Action Reports
indicators.statement: Incident after action reports are generated and lessons learned are persistently incorporated.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Incident Response Procedures
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of documented incident response procedures is persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Past Incidents
indicators.statement: Past incidents are persistently reviewed for patterns or vulnerabilities that were not previously apparent or identified.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.
Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Evaluating Configurations
indicators.statement: The configuration of machine-based information resources, especially infrastructure as code, is persistently evaluated and tested.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Logging Event Types
indicators.statement: A list of information resources and event types that will be logged, monitored, and audited is maintained and persistently reviewed to ensure these activities occur.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Operating SIEM Capability
indicators.statement: A Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) or similar system(s) is used and persistently reviewed for centralized, tamper-resistant logging of events, activities, and changes.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Logs
indicators.statement: Logs are persistently reviewed and audited.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Generating Inventories
indicators.statement: Authoritative sources are used to automatically generate real-time inventories of all information resources when needed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Executive Support
indicators.statement: Executive support for achieving the provider's security goals is persistently reviewed and demonstrated.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Investments in Security
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of the provider's investments in achieving security goals is persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Security in the SDLC
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of building security and privacy considerations into the Software Development Lifecycle and aligning with CISA Secure By Design principles is persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Vulnerability Disclosures
indicators.statement: The effectiveness of the provider's vulnerability disclosure program is persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Aligning Backups with Objectives
indicators.statement: The alignment of machine-based information resource backups with defined recovery objectives is persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Aligning Recovery Plan
indicators.statement: The alignment of recovery plans with defined recovery objectives is persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Reviewing Recovery Objectives
indicators.statement: The desired Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) are defined and persistently reviewed for alignment with the provider's business needs and capabilities.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Testing Recovery Capabilities
indicators.statement: The capability to recover from incidents and contingencies aligned with defined recovery objectives is persistently tested.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Mitigating Supply Chain Risk
indicators.statement: Persistently identify, review, and mitigate potential supply chain risks.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Monitoring Supply Chain Risk
indicators.statement: Third party software information resources are automatically monitored for upstream vulnerabilities using mechanisms that may include contractual notification requirements or active monitoring services.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Automating Configuration Management
indicators.statement: The configuration of machine-based information resources is managed using automation and persistently reviewed for drift.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Automating Secret Management
indicators.statement: Management, protection, and regular rotation of digital keys, certificates, and other secrets is automated and persistently reviewed.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Evaluating and Improving Security
indicators.statement: Information resources are persistently evaluated for opportunities to improve security and those improvements are persistently made.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.
Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.
Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Securing Information
indicators.statement: Information is encrypted or otherwise secured from unwanted access or modification.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.
Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

indicators.name: Validating Resource Integrity
indicators.statement: Use cryptographic methods to validate the integrity of machine-based information resources.

updated.comment: Official launch of the FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026.

vendors.product: Microsoft 365 Government Community Cloud High (GCC High)
vendors.cui_suitability: Appropriate for CUI under DFARS 7012; sovereign tenancy
vendors.notes: Rev5 agency path. FedRAMP 20x Class packages are emerging; verify path on Marketplace before SSP citation.
ksi_due_diligence: Confirm Marketplace package FR1824057433 authorization status is Active
Obtain CRM Appendix J for tenant-specific inherited controls
Verify trust-center or compliance page documents FedRAMP authorization scope
Map CRM 800-53 rows to CMMC requirements via references/data/800-53-crosswalk.json

vendors.cui_suitability: Source-code-only low-risk SaaS; not a general CUI file store
vendors.notes: Public trust page is a model for FedRAMP 20x certification data sharing expectations.
ksi_due_diligence: Confirm Tailored/LI-SaaS scope matches source-code-only use
Review public trust page for certification data API or programmatic access docs
Verify no CUI in issues, PR bodies, or attachments outside design intent

vendors.cui_suitability: Appropriate for CUI under DFARS 7012; per-model GovCloud availability
vendors.notes: Bedrock inherits AWS GovCloud boundary; not a standalone Marketplace package.
ksi_due_diligence: Confirm platform package F1603047866 authorization on Marketplace
Verify per-model Bedrock availability in GovCloud documentation
Obtain CRM for inherited platform controls; customer configures model access
